channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As western fires erupt, Trump's Forest Service says it's now fully staffed
item.description: The Forest Service says it's fully staffed with seasonal firefighters going into summer but there are still questions about whether the government is prepared if major wildfires get out of hand.
item.content:encoded:
The Forest Service says it's fully staffed with seasonal firefighters going into summer but there are still questions about whether the government is prepared if major wildfires get out of hand.

(Image credit: Kirk Siegler)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Here's how much the Iran war cost -- and how its effects will linger
item.description: More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.
item.content:encoded:
More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.

(Image credit: Firdous Nazir)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: An inside look at President Trump's campaign to acquire Greenland
item.description: New Yorker writer Ben Taub says while the idea of acquiring Greenland is out of the headlines, it hasn't been dropped. Taub describes how Trump's ongoing efforts have broken the trust of our allies.
item.content:encoded: New Yorker writer Ben Taub says while the idea of acquiring Greenland is out of the headlines, it hasn't been dropped. Taub describes how Trump's ongoing efforts have broken the trust of our allies.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur is the first named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season
item.description: Forecasters say Arthur could generate life-threatening flash floods along the northern Gulf Coast. But it is not expected to strengthen further.
item.content:encoded: Forecasters say Arthur could generate life-threatening flash floods along the northern Gulf Coast. But it is not expected to strengthen further.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Greetings from Maputo, Mozambique's capital, shaped by a modernist architecture
item.description: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.content:encoded: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: Pakistan ends 'luxury tax' on menstrual products, contraceptives. Will prices drop?
item.description: In Pakistan, taxes on menstrual products can add up. Activists have long worked to change this. Now a new budget wipes out the 18% sales tax. But questions remain about the impact on prices.
item.content:encoded:
In Pakistan, taxes on menstrual products can add up. Activists have long worked to change this. Now a new budget wipes out the 18% sales tax. But questions remain about the impact on prices.

(Image credit: Huizeng Hu/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Taiwan says Chinese pressure over the island is the 'new normal'
item.description: Taiwan's Foreign Ministry on Tuesday said the scholars' passports and mobile phones were confiscated, and they were detained in Mombasa for more than 20 hours before being allowed to leave the country.
item.content:encoded:
Taiwan's Foreign Ministry on Tuesday said the scholars' passports and mobile phones were confiscated, and they were detained in Mombasa for more than 20 hours before being allowed to leave the country.

(Image credit: Brian Inganga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate postpones confirmation hearing for intel chief after Trump's call to delay
item.description: Senators wanted to fast-track President Trump's pick for Director of National Intelligence. But Trump now says he wants to delay until they pass voting legislation that already failed in the Senate.
item.content:encoded:
Senators wanted to fast-track President Trump's pick for Director of National Intelligence. But Trump now says he wants to delay until they pass voting legislation that already failed in the Senate.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Trump to face questions at G7 press conference. And, Tuesday's primary results
item.description: Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.
item.content:encoded:
Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.

(Image credit: Ludovix Marin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'Rejected': How federal prisons stonewall grievances and deny care for years
item.description: People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.
item.content:encoded:
People who go to prison keep one important right — to file a grievance over their treatment: from abuse to denied medical care. But in the vast majority of cases, those efforts go nowhere, according to an analysis of federal data by The Marshall Project and NPR.

(Image credit: Michelle Mildenberg Lara for The Marshall Project)
item.dc:creator: Christie Thompson
